summary: ARCH-001 accepted the boundary: CLI owns local contract/claim/execute/ack/finish orchestration; API owns lifecycle/job mutation.
Current CLI scope already contains the smallest next unit: finish the behavior-preserving F365-F368 helper/service split and focused test relocation.
The executor todo must stay entirely inside packages/cli/src; no API files, docs files, daemon behavior, or public default changes.
Focused verification should use explicit task-pull test matching, plus CLI type-check/build and line-count hygiene.
why_next: This is the next smallest CLI step because F365-F368 define already-scoped, behavior-preserving CLI helper boundaries and the current worktree contains exactly those CLI-side production/test changes. It closes the active CLI split before any new behavior, keeps once/loop/executor responsibilities narrow, and avoids overlap with API lifecycle work already handled by API-owned F369+ slices.
risks: Untracked helper/test files must be included by the executor; otherwise imports or coverage relocation can be incomplete.
A fuzzy Jest selector can accidentally include *.test-utils.ts; use explicit --testMatch.
Moving assertions out of larger specs can hide coverage loss if the executor deletes rather than relocates cases.
CLI lint remains a known separate blocker if the existing ESLint config issue is still present.
